figures.caption: Table 1: Robotic manipulation results on RoboCasa [33].

Figure 1: (a) In-context imitation learning (ICIL) [13] allows robots to generalize from demonstrations to new tasks without retraining. Action tokenizer (AT) is important to capturing demonstration information effectively. (b) Previous AT methods (e.g., [27]) rely on vector quantization, conducting flat clustering and focusing on spatial cues via recovering input actions. (c) We propose a hierarchical spatiotemporal action tokenizer, which performs multi-level clustering and exploits both spatial and temporal cues by jointly reconstructing actions and timestamps, yielding superior performance.
Table 2: Robotic manipulation results on ManiSkill [34].
Table 7: Zero-shot robotic manipulation results on RoboCasa [33].
Figure 2: An overview of our hierarchical spatiotemporal action tokenizer (HiST-AT).
